column4: Description
Child taps signs (e.g., arrows, emojis) in response to meaningful cues.
Child taps print symbols embedded in meaningful signs (e.g., stop, go, up, down)
Child taps printed words corresponding to words embedded in signs.
Child taps sentence to initiate read aloud.
Child swipes words left to right to initiate read aloud.
Child taps letters among letters and non-letters.
1. Child sees letter. 2. Child taps specific letter among other non-target letters
1. Child told letter name. 2. Child taps letter named among other non-named letters.
Child follows steps for tracing letters.
1. Child sees letter. 2. Child told letter name. 3. Child follows steps for tracing letters while saying letter name.
1. Child sees letter and letter disappears. 2. Child writes letter and says its name.
1. Child told letter name. 2. Child writes letter from memory and says letter name.
Child sees letter, says its name.
1. Child prompted to say any letter. 2. Multiple letters appear. 3. Child taps said letter.
Child swipes common endings of rhyming wods.
column9: 3 flowers with two petals where each petal speaks a syllable (state-ment, fig-ment, na-ture) when touched. Player must cut (swipe) and collect the rhyming petals (ment).
column12: [state-ment, fig-ment, na-ture] [sta-tion, fic-tion, pos-ture] [cap-ture, struc-ture, cen-sor] [aw-ful, ruf-fle, strug-gle]

column4: Child swipes set of words that do not match.
column9: 3 rows of 3 mats (with rows in between rows) where each mat speaks a phoneme when stood on. Player must step on each row (e.g., /bet/ /bat/ /bat/). Player must remove (swipe) the mats of row that doesn't match.
column12: [bet, bat, bat] [sit, sit, lit] [stale, stale, sale] [best, best, bets]

column4: Child taps rhyming syllables
column9: 4 speaking stones say the words "cap" "cat" "nap" "pan" when touched. Player must drag the rhyming stones into their rhyme bag.
column12: [cap, cat, nap, pan] [wish, dish, fish, chip] [slam, cram, slip, slot] [table, cable, nation, belly] [wiggle, squiggle, tickle, shuffle]

column4: Child taps sounds in a sequence.
column9: 3 speaking stones say the phonemes /k/ /p/ /f/ when touched. Player is given oral instructions to tap the following sequence: /p/ /p/ /k/ /f/
column13: Can be non-word sequence, nonsense word sequences, or real word sequences

column4: Child hears multisyllable word. 2. Child taps the number of syllables.
column9: Hidden doorway whispers the code "enfogment". Player must tap the number of syllables in the word (3).
column12: smaggle (2), shneak (1), discrete (2), misdirection (4), invisible (4), intentionally (5)

column4: Child hears single syllable word. 2. Child taps the number of sounds.
column9: Magical lock whispers the word "hid". Player must tap the number of phonemes (3).

column4: Child hears multiple syllables spoken one at a time. Child blends them together.
column9: Two-way gramophone plays skipping syllables: /oat/ (pause) /meal/. Player must say syllables together in succession ("oatmeal").
column12: oatmeal, backyard, subject, unless, subjecting, recording, dismissive, unwittingly

column4: Child hears multiple phonemes spoken one at at time. Child blends them together.
column9: Two-way gramophone plays skipping sounds: /k/ (pause) /a/ (pause) /t/. Player must say sounds together in succession ("cat").
column12: up, if, can, shun, clip, smile, sneak, pound, sleeps

column4: Child hears multisyllable word. 2. Child taps space representing first syllable and speaks syllable. 3. Child taps space representing next syllable and speaks syllabe. (and so on).
column9: Glowing jewels on a crown speak out the word "bedazzle". Player must tap first jewel and say "be", then tap second jewel and say "daz", then tap final jewel and say "zle".

column4: Child hears single syllable word. 2. Child taps space representing first sound and speaks sound. 3. Child taps space representing next sound and speaks sound. (and so on).
column9: Glowing links on a chain speak out the word "stash". Player must tap first link and say /s/, then tap second link and say /t/, then tap third link and say /a/, then tap final link and say /sh/.

column4: Child hears single syllable word. Child sees visual modification representing phoneme deletion. Child says new word.
column9: Glowing links on a chain speak out the word "slab". The first chain falls off. Player must speak new word ("lab").
column12: slab-> lab, cat->at, bent->bet, sharks->shark, snaps->naps
column13: Use real words to start, deletions can be nonsense words

column4: Child hears single syllable word. Child sees visual modification representing phoneme addition. Child says new word.
column9: Glowing links on a chain speak out the word "cup". A new chain making the /s/ sound is added after the /u/. Player must speak new word ("cusp").
column12: cup -> cusp, fan -> fans, ban -> band, snack->snacks, sale -> scale
column13: Use real words to start, additions can be nonsense words

column4: Child hears single syllable word. Child sees visual modification representing phoneme transposition. Child says new word.

column1: Alphabetic Principle (Integrating Phonological Awarenes with Decoding)
column4: 1. Child hears sound. 2. Child says sound. 3. Child sees said letter.
1. Child hears sound. 2. Child sees letters. 3. Child taps letter associated with said sound.
column9: 4 gems each with an inscribed letter (g, b, t, k). A voice speaks /b/. Child prompted to tap the gem with 'g'.
column13: All letters except q needs to be qu

column4: 1. Child hears sound. 2. Child writes letter.
column9: The fortress is under a sonic attack. Various sound missles can be stopped by writing the letters representing them. A soundmissle of a single phoneme /s/ grows louder until player writes the player writes 's'.

column4: 1. Child hears sound. 2. Child writes letter of said sound and says letter name.
column9: Same as above, but says letter while writing (maybe making the spell more powerful? killing multiple missles?)

column4: 1. Child says sound. 2. Child sees said letter.
1. Child prompted to say any sound. 2. Multiple letters appear. 3. Child taps letter associated with said sound.
1. Child sees letter. 2. Child says sound.
1. Child sees letter. 2. Child traces letter and says letter name. 3. Child says letter sound.
1. Child sees letter and letter disappears. 2. Child writes letter from memory and says name. 3. Child says letter sound.
1. Child sees word. 2. Child traces letters and says letter names, one at a time. 3. Child says word.
1. Child sees word. 2. Child taps letters and says associated sounds. 3. Child swipes word to blend sounds.
1. Child sees word. 2. Letters vanish. 3. Child taps spaces and says associated sounds. 4. Child swipes word to blend sounds.
1. Child see words. 2. Child traces letter. 3. Letters vanish. 4. Child reads word.
column9: A spellbook contains a set of words of valuable items. The player is prompted to trace the letters and they vanish after they are traced. After they are traced, player reads the word that disappeared to conjure up an item.
column13: Use real words or fake words that refer to items built into narrative

column4: 1. Child sees word. 2. Child reads word.

column3: See, Swipe, Trace, Say, Trace, Say, Say
column4: 1. Child sees word. 2. Child breaks syllables. 3. Child traces letter in syllable. 4. Child reads syllable. 5. Child traces letter in other syllable. 6. Child reads syllable. 7. Child blends syllables.
1. Child sees word. 2. Child breaks syllables. 3. Child reads word.
1. Child sees word. 2. Child splits syllables. 3. Child writes letters and says letter names. 4. Child reads syllables. 5. Child reads word.
1. Child sees word. 2. Child splits syllables. 3. Child reads syllables. 4. Child reads words.
1. Child sees word 2. Child says one letter at a time.
1. Child sees and hears a word. 2. Child says a sound. 3. Letter animates in response. 4. Child drags the letter away from word.
1. Child sees word and hears word. 2. Child hears first sound. 3. Child writes first sound. 4. Child hears second sound. 5. Child writes second sound.
1. Child hears a word. 2. Child writes letters.
column9: Player hears an item calling. Player spells the letters of the item to summon it.
column12: Moth, Axe, Broom, Horse, Snake, Butterfly
column13: Use real words or fake words that refer to items built into narrative
